mission.title: keda: Respect to cooldownPeriod for the first deployment and let service is up and and running based on replica number for the first time.
mission.description: Respect to cooldownPeriod for the first deployment and let service is up and and running based on replica number for the first time. . Requested by 7+ users.

steps.title: Check current keda deployment
steps.description:
Verify your keda version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n keda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keda
helm list -n keda 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working keda installation.

steps.title: Review keda configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant keda configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n keda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keda
kubectl get configmap -n keda -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=keda
```
### Proposal

Hey 
From my understanding based on the current documentation, the cooldownPeriod in KEDA only takes effect after a scaling trigger has occurred.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Respect to cooldownPeriod for the first deployment and let…
steps.description:
… of CooldownPeriod

·

Add a new field InitialDelayCooldownPeriod The purpose of adding a new field InitialDelayCooldownPeriod is to delay the effective time of the CooldownPeriod, so that when the service is started for the first time, it needs to wait for the time of InitialDelayCooldownPeriod
```yaml
spec:
  advanced:
    horizontalPodAutoscalerConfig:
      behavior:
        scaleDown:
          policies:
          - periodSeconds: 300
            type: Pods
            value: 1
          stabilizationWindowSeconds: 1800
        scaleUp:
          policies:
          - periodSeconds: 300
            type: Percent
            value: 100
          stabilizationWindowSeconds: 0
    restoreToOriginalReplicaCount: true
  cooldownPeriod: 1800
  fallback:
    failureThreshold: 3
    replicas: 1
  maxReplicaCount: 10
  minReplicaCount: 0
  pollingInterval: 20
  scaleTargetRef:
    name: test
  tr
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n keda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keda
kubectl get events -n keda --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Respect to cooldownPeriod for the first deployment and let…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
… of CooldownPeriod

·

Add a new field InitialDelayCooldownPeriod The purpose of adding a new field InitialDelayCooldownPeriod is to delay the effective time of the CooldownPeriod, so that when the service is started for the first time, it needs to wait for the time of InitialDelayCooldownPeriod to expire before destroying the service.

Relates to #

spec:
  advanced:
    horizontalPodAutoscalerConfig:
      behavior:
        scaleDown:
          policies:
          - periodSeconds: 300
            type: Pods
            value: 1
          stabilizationWindowSeconds: 1800
        scaleUp:
          policies:
          - periodSeconds: 300
            type: Percent
            value: 100
          stabilizationWindowSeconds: 0
    restoreToOriginalReplicaCount: true
  cooldownPeriod: 1800
  fallback:
    failureThreshold: 3
    replicas: 1
  maxReplicaCount: 10
  minReplicaCount: 0
  pollingInterval: 20
  scaleTargetRef:
    name: test
  triggers:
  - authenticationRef:
      name: test
    metadata:
      mode: QueueLength
      protocol: amqp
      queueName: test
      value: "150"
    type: rabbitmq

kubectl describe ScaledObject -n test
Normal  KEDAScalersStarted          5s   keda-operator  Started scalers watch
Normal  ScaledObjectReady           5s   keda-operator  ScaledObject is ready for scaling
Normal  KEDAScaleTargetDeactivated  5s   keda-operator  Deactivated apps/v1.Deployment test/test from 1 to 0

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with keda installed or the issue environment reproducible.
